data.description:
## Motivation
Discovered from 4de6 (completion verify->sign TOCTOU) and 80af (--ref carrier). rebar's git-tree-reading GATES (completion verification, plan-review claim gate's code reads, code review, commit-ticket verify) resolve symbolic refs (HEAD / origin/main) LAZILY and sometimes MORE THAN ONCE within a single logical operation. Any re-resolution of a symbolic ref mid-operation is a TOCTOU seam: a concurrent commit (routine in the parallel-agent/shared-worktree model) splits the two reads. 4de6 is the concrete instance; the --ref carrier fix pins the completion close, but the pattern is broader.

## Pattern to adopt
"Resolve once, thread the concrete sha." A symbolic ref is resolved to an IMMUTABLE sha ONCE at an operation's entry; every tree read within that operation uses that sha (never re-resolves the symbol). rebar already has both halves: the _snapshot/gate_source cache (snapshots keyed by sha -> free consistency across reads at the same sha) and the --ref param carrier (80af). The remaining work is to route EVERY gate's tree reads through a per-operation resolved sha, and to audit for any remaining lazy re-resolution seams (e.g. completion close also re-reads via caused-by git-blame and the plan-review close-gate recheck).

## Optional: CLI-scoped 'pinned rev'
Mirroring config.set_cli_overrides (the -c layer set once at main() entry and read by all consumers) and the ad9f central mount gate, a contextvars pinned-rev could be set once at CLI entry (resolve_ref('HEAD')) and serve as the DEFAULT source of 'what HEAD means now' for gates that default to HEAD.

## Caveats (why NOT a single invocation-wide rev)
1. Different gates have different natural base refs: completion close verifies HEAD (the tree being closed); plan-review/code-review verify the ATTESTED base (origin/main via REBAR_GATE_REF/[snapshot].ref). One invocation-wide pin would force them onto one rev -> wrong for at least one. Correct granularity is resolve-once-PER-OPERATION (each op pins ITS ref), with any CLI pin acting only as the default HEAD-sha source, not a single global rev.
2. Library/MCP have no single 'invocation' boundary (long-lived processes call many ops over time) -> ambient pinning is a CLI concept only; library/MCP callers thread ref explicitly (or None for lazy). Same CLI-only split as the ad9f mount gate.

## Scope sketch (children, if promoted)
- Audit all gate tree-reads for lazy/multiple symbolic-ref resolution; enumerate seams.
- Per-operation resolve-once for each gate (completion already via 4de6; plan-review; code-review; commit-ticket).
- Optional CLI-scoped pinned-rev contextvar + default wiring (CLI only).
- Tests: for each gate, moving HEAD mid-operation must not split verify from its downstream (sign / hash / compare).
data.title: Invocation-consistent git-rev pinning: resolve-once-per-operation + optional CLI-scoped pinned-rev for all tree-reading gates
